title: Hieracium lasiothrix
intro: Hieracium lasiothrix, also known as Rough Hawkweed, is a perennial plant species within the dandelion family (Asteraceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it beneficial for treating various health conditions such as wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium lasiothrix include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat a range of health issues, although further research is necessary to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
